summary: Large language models (LLMs) are typically pretrained on short sequences and then extended to work on longer sequences with additional training. However, such LLMs still struggle to further generalize to very long sequences. We propose Randomized YaRN, a training method that improves length generalization by combining YaRN-based positional extrapolation with randomized positional encoding and a length curriculum. During training on short context data, tokens are assigned YaRN positional encodings sampled from a larger position range, exposing the model to out-of-distribution positional representations even on short-context inputs. We evaluate Randomized YaRN on two challenging long-context reasoning benchmarks, BABILong and Multi-Round Coreference Resolution (MRCR). When training on data with <8K context, Randomized YaRN consistently improves reasoning performance on context lengths from 16K to 128K and outperforms standard fine-tuning, with the largest gains appearing at far out-of-distribution lengths. Our results suggest that progressively exposing models to OOD positional distributions provides an effective recipe for generalizable long-context reasoning.
title: Randomized YaRN Improves Length Generalization for Long-Context Reasoning
title_translated: 随机化YaRN提升长上下文推理的长度泛化能力
summary_translated: 大型语言模型（LLMs）通常在短序列上进行预训练，然后通过额外训练扩展到更长的序列。然而，这类模型在进一步泛化到极长序列时仍存在困难。我们提出随机化YaRN，一种通过结合基于YaRN的位置外推与随机位置编码及长度课程训练的方法，以改善长度泛化。在短上下文数据训练期间，令牌被分配来自更大位置范围的YaRN位置编码，使模型即使在短上下文输入中也能接触到分布外的位置表示。我们在两个具有挑战性的长上下文推理基准测试BABILong和多轮共指消解（MRCR）上评估了随机化YaRN。当在<8K上下文的数据上训练时，随机化YaRN在16K至128K上下文长度上持续提升了推理性能，并优于标准微调，尤其在远超分布长度时表现最佳。结果表明，逐步向模型暴露分布外的位置分布是一种实现可泛化长上下文推理的有效策略。

summary: Prior work shows that large language models (LLMs) exhibit introspective capability on benign tasks. We extend the question to safety contexts and examine how reliably a model can recognize that its own prior response was elicited by an adversarial prefill attack. Across ten open-weight instruction-tuned LLMs (3B to 70B) and four safety benchmarks, no model reliably recognizes its own compromised outputs, with models claiming intent on prefilled responses at an average rate of $27.3\%$. Introspective signal stems largely from safety- and refusal-related reasoning. Orthogonalizing models' weights against the refusal direction collapses the gap between claiming rates on prefilled and natural outputs to near zero, though the direction is not its unique mediator. The signal is also probe-dependent: framing the question as internal intention versus external tampering elicits qualitatively different responses on the same models. We test three LoRA finetuning methods (SFT, GRPO, DPO) on eight models from 3B to 27B; all three widen the intention-probe gap on every model from 8B to 27B, with method ranking varying by model. The intervention does not transfer to the tampering probe and counterintuitively raises attack success rate under adversarial prefill on most models, amounting to a partial mitigation. These findings outline mechanisms underpinning the observed introspective signals in safety contexts and highlight risks in the reliability of LLM self-reports.
title: Can LLMs Reliably Self-Report Adversarial Prefills, and How?
title_translated: 大语言模型能否可靠地自我报告对抗性前填充攻击？以及如何做到？
summary_translated: 先前研究表明，大型语言模型（LLMs）在良性任务中表现出内省能力。我们将这一问题拓展至安全场景，研究模型在多大程度上能识别自身先前响应是被对抗性前填充攻击所诱导的。在十种开放权重的指令微调LLM（3B至70B）和四个安全基准测试中，没有一个模型能可靠地识别其受损输出，模型在前填充响应中声称意图的平均比例为27.3%。内省信号主要源于安全性和拒绝相关推理。将模型权重对拒绝方向正交化后，前填充与自然输出之间的声称率差距几乎归零，尽管该方向并非唯一中介。信号还依赖于探测方式：将问题表述为内部意图与外部篡改，会引发同一模型产生质性不同的回答。我们在八种3B至27B的模型上测试了三种LoRA微调方法（SFT、GRPO、DPO）；所有三种方法均在每个8B至27B的模型上扩大了意图探测的差距，且方法排名随模型变化。该干预无法迁移至篡改探测，且反直觉地提高了大多数模型在对抗性前填充下的攻击成功率，仅构成部分缓解。这些发现揭示了安全情境下观察到的内省信号背后的机制，并突显了LLM自我报告可靠性方面的风险。

summary: Enterprise agents increasingly operate inside workspaces: they read heterogeneous files, invoke tools, and deliver business artifacts. We introduce EnterpriseClawBench, an enterprise agent benchmark constructed from proprietary, real-world agent sessions. Starting from a large archive of workplace sessions, the EnterpriseClawBench produces 852 reproducible tasks, each paired with recovered fixtures, rewritten prompts, role classes, skill subclasses, hard rules, and semantic rubrics. Because the sessions contain internal enterprise content, we do not release the benchmark data; instead, our reusable contribution is the construction and evaluation protocol. On EnterpriseClawBench, the best configuration reaches only 0.663 (Codex with GPT-5.5). These results show that enterprise agent evaluation must report harness--model combinations, artifact delivery, visual quality, cost, runtime, and skill-transfer behavior, rather than collapsing performance into a single score. Code: https://github.com/FrontisAI/EnterpriseClawBench
title: EnterpriseClawBench: Benchmarking Agents from Real Workplace Sessions
title_translated: EnterpriseClawBench：基于真实工作场景会话的企业智能体基准测试
summary_translated: 企业智能体越来越多地在工作空间中运行：它们读取异构文件、调用工具并交付业务成果。我们引入EnterpriseClawBench，一个基于专有真实企业智能体会话构建的企业智能体基准测试。从大量工作场所会话档案出发，EnterpriseClawBench生成了852个可复现的任务，每个任务均配有恢复的固定条件、重写提示、角色类别、技能子类、硬规则和语义评分标准。由于会话包含内部企业内容，我们不公开基准数据；我们的可复用贡献在于构建与评估协议。在EnterpriseClawBench上，最佳配置仅达到0.663分（Codex搭配GPT-5.5）。这些结果表明，企业智能体评估必须报告测试框架-模型组合、成果交付、视觉质量、成本、运行时间及技能迁移行为，而非将其简化为单一分数。

summary: Safety benchmarks assume that test-condition behavior predicts deployment behavior, an assumption that fails if models detect evaluation cues and adapt. This opens a gap between benchmark performance and deployment behavior: compliance measured under test conditions becomes an optimistic upper bound that overstates how safely a model behaves once the evaluation harness is removed. We characterize this evaluation awareness through eight experiments across 37 open-weight models and seven families. (i)Detection is moderate and training-driven (24/37 models exceed chance, best AUROC 0.714 vs.0.819 human, with instruction tuning dominating over scale). (ii)Detection shifts safety behavior (hard refusal drops 5.8 percentage points under hypothetical framing, and 21/140 HarmBench framing effects are significant, with compliance rising up to +30 percentage points. (iii)Representations survive behavioral collapse (probes retain AUROC 0.98 under rewrites that drive behavior below chance, and multi-layer steering causally moves three downstream tasks while random controls do not). (iv)These axes are weakly coupled (only 1/15 correlations are significant, the sole robust link being behavioral detection versus framing resistance, $ρ=-0.79$, $p<0.001$). We call this gap the benchmark illusion: because detectability, behavioral manifestation, and controllability vary independently, it is multivariate rather than a single number, so no single awareness score is a reliable proxy for deployment safety.
title: Evaluation Awareness Is Not One Capability: Evidence from Open Language Models
title_translated: 评估意识并非单一能力：来自开源语言模型的证据
summary_translated: 安全基准测试假设测试条件下的行为可预测部署行为，但若模型能检测评估线索并作出适应，则该假设失效。这导致基准性能与实际部署行为之间出现差距：在测试条件下测得的合规性成为乐观上界，高估了模型在移除评估框架后的安全性。我们通过在37个开源权重模型和七个模型家族上的八项实验来刻画这种评估意识。（i）检测能力中等且受训练驱动（37个模型中有24个超过随机水平，最佳AUROC为0.714 vs. 人类0.819，指令微调的影响大于规模）。（ii）检测会改变安全行为（在假设性表述下，强硬拒绝下降5.8个百分点；140次HarmBench表述效应中21次显著，合规性最高上升30个百分点）。（iii）表征在行为崩溃后仍存留（在使行为低于随机水平的重写下，探测仍保持AUROC 0.98；多层操控可因果影响三个下游任务，而随机控制则不能）。（iv）这些维度弱相关（15个相关性中仅1个显著，唯一稳健关联为行为检测与表述抗性，ρ=-0.79，p<0.001）。我们称此差距为基准幻觉：由于可检测性、行为表现和可控性独立变化，它是一个多维现象，而非单一数值，因此不存在可靠的单一意识得分作为部署安全的代理。

summary: Language-specific tokenizers improve tokenization quality and the downstream performance of models on those languages. However, using such a tokenizer comes at a cost: either a new model must be trained from scratch, or the vocabulary of an existing pretrained model must be adapted. We propose Language-adaptive Maximum a Posteriori (LangMAP) Tokenization, a tokenization scheme that extends the UnigramLM algorithm to the multilingual setting, producing language-specific tokenization from a single shared vocabulary. Notably, LangMAP can be used when training a multilingual language model from scratch or to adapt a pretrained model's tokenizer to individual languages without changing its vocabulary. While language labels are required at training time, a key feature of the algorithm is that it then performs language-specific tokenization at inference without knowledge of the input's language. Across 14 open-source tokenizers, 9 natural languages, and 9 programming languages, LangMAP improves morphological boundary alignment and, for all coding languages tested, alignment with abstract syntax tree (AST) leaf boundaries. In fine-tuning experiments, results are mixed: LangMAP improves target-language grammatical acceptability (MultiBLiMP) on the languages tested; its benefits are less consistent on knowledge-related tasks (Global-PIQA, Belebele).
title: LangMAP: A Language-Adaptive Approach to Tokenization
title_translated: LangMAP：一种语言自适应的分词方法
summary_translated: 特定语言的分词器能提升模型在该语言上的分词质量和下游性能。然而，使用此类分词器需付出代价：要么必须从头训练新模型，要么必须调整现有预训练模型的词汇表。我们提出语言自适应最大后验（LangMAP）分词法，一种将UnigramLM算法扩展至多语言环境的分词方案，仅通过单一共享词汇表即可生成语言特定的分词。值得注意的是，LangMAP可在从头训练多语言语言模型时使用，也可用于在不更改词汇表的情况下将预训练模型的分词器适配到单个语言。虽然训练时需要语言标签，但该算法的关键特性是在推理阶段无需知道输入语言即可执行语言特定分词。在14个开源分词器、9种自然语言和9种编程语言上的实验表明，LangMAP提升了形态边界对齐效果，且在所有测试的编程语言中均实现了与抽象语法树（AST）叶节点边界的更好对齐。在微调实验中，结果参差不齐：LangMAP在测试语言的语法可接受性（MultiBLiMP）上有所提升；但在知识相关任务（Global-PIQA、Belebele）上的收益不够一致。

summary: Long agent traces composed of chains of thought and tool calls accumulate stale content that anchor subsequent generations, and eventually outgrow the context window. Existing scaffolds mitigate it with fixed-interval compaction triggered at a token threshold. Such triggers pay no heed to trajectory structure, risking discard of partial results mid-derivation or mid-search. We propose SelfCompact, a scaffold that allows the model itself to decide when and how to compact. Specifically, it pairs two inference-time elements: (i) a compaction tool the model invokes to summarize the accumulated context, and (ii) a lightweight rubric specifying when to fire (a sub-task has resolved, or the trajectory is converging) and when to suppress (mid-derivation, or when stuck). Both are needed. The tool alone is unevenly used across open-weight models, often invoked at unhelpful moments or not at all; the rubric alone cannot act. Together, they elicit effective adaptive compaction without any fine-tuning or external supervision. We present empirical results on six benchmarks (competitive math and agentic search) and seven models. Our results show that SelfCompact matches or exceeds fixed-interval summarization at a fraction of the token cost, improving over a no-summarization baseline by up to 18.1 points on math and 5-9 points on agentic search at 30-70% lower per-question cost. Our results expose a meta-cognitive gap: although unprompted models cannot reliably tell when their own context is rotting, a lightweight rubric closes this gap, reframing when to compact as a capability that scaffolds can supply without training.
title: Self-Compacting Language Model Agents
title_translated: 自压缩语言模型智能体
summary_translated: 由思维链和工具调用组成的长智能体轨迹会积累过时内容，这些内容会锚定后续生成结果，并最终超出上下文窗口。现有框架通过在达到固定token阈值时触发的间隔压缩来缓解此问题。然而，这类触发机制不考虑轨迹结构，可能导致在推导或搜索过程中部分结果被错误丢弃。我们提出SelfCompact，一种允许模型自身决定何时以及如何压缩的框架。具体而言，它结合了两个推理时元素：(i) 模型调用的压缩工具，用于总结累积的上下文；(ii) 一个轻量级规则，指定触发条件（子任务已解决或轨迹趋于收敛）和抑制条件（处于推导中途或卡住时）。两者缺一不可。仅使用工具会导致在不同开源模型中使用不均，常在无益时刻调用或完全不调用；仅使用规则则无法执行操作。二者结合可在无需微调或外部监督的情况下实现有效的自适应压缩。我们在六个基准测试（包括数学竞赛和智能体搜索）和七个模型上进行了实证研究。结果表明，SelfCompact以极低的token成本实现了与固定间隔摘要相当甚至更优的表现，在数学任务上相比无摘要基线最高提升18.1分，在智能体搜索任务上提升5-9分，且每题成本降低30%-70%。研究揭示了一个元认知差距：尽管未经提示的模型无法可靠判断自身上下文是否已失效，但一个轻量级规则可弥补这一差距，将压缩时机视为可通过框架提供而无需训练的能力。

summary: Scientists do not, by profession, wage war. Yet warfare's vocabulary consistently appears in their abstracts. To quantify the extent to which warfare's vocabulary pervades scientific abstracts, we analyze 21.4 million papers (2010-2025; OpenAlex, PubMed). We additionally run a within-subject war-framing experiment (N = 801; 32,040 trials) designed to provide causal insight into the effects of militaristic language on persuasion. Between 2010 and 2025, the presence of militaristic terms in scientific abstracts rose 48% in OpenAlex and 32% in PubMed, with the rise accelerating sharply after 2019 (cross-database r = 0.96, p < 10^-8). The prevalence of militaristic language is conflict-aligned at both country and annual scales (Uppsala Conflict Data Program; r = 0.77-0.84), with the abstracts from the Global South displaying the fastest rise in militaristic language. Among disciplines, social sciences leads in level of such language while engineering and computer science lead in growth. The COVID and post-2022 large-language-model eras also saw the rise and narrowed the language gap between native-English and non-English authors. In our follow-up experiment, we found that war framing reduced credibility (mean shift -0.18 Likert units, 95% CI [-0.21, -0.14]; d_z = -0.28, p < 10^-20), funding willingness (d_z = -0.12) and policy support (d_z = -0.08), with a trend-level increase in sense of urgency (d_z = +0.07). Collectively, findings reveal that while scientific abstracts drift toward warfare, the use of militaristic language may erode credibility, funding willingness, and policy support.
title: War in the Abstract: The Rise and Consequences of Militarized Language in Scientific Communication
title_translated: 抽象中的战争：军事化语言在科学传播中的兴起及其后果
summary_translated: 科学家本职并非参战，但其论文摘要中却持续出现战争词汇。为量化军事化语言在科学摘要中的渗透程度，我们分析了2140万篇论文（2010–2025年；OpenAlex、PubMed）。此外，我们还开展了一项被试内战争框架实验（N=801；32,040次试验），旨在揭示军事化语言对说服力的因果影响。2010至2025年间，科学摘要中军事化术语的出现率在OpenAlex上升48%，在PubMed上升32%，且自2019年后增速显著加快（跨数据库相关系数r=0.96，p<10^-8）。军事化语言的流行程度在国家和年度层面均与冲突情况高度一致（Uppsala冲突数据项目；r=0.77–0.84），其中全球南方地区的上升速度最快。在学科领域中，社会科学的军事化语言水平最高，而工程学和计算机科学的增长最快。新冠疫情及2022年后大语言模型时代也推动了军事化语言的兴起，并缩小了英语母语与非英语作者之间的语言差距。在后续实验中，我们发现战争框架降低了可信度（平均下降0.18李克特单位，95%置信区间[-0.21, -0.14]；d_z = -0.28，p < 10^-20）、资金支持意愿（d_z = -0.12）和政策支持度（d_z = -0.08），同时显示出紧迫感略有上升的趋势（d_z = +0.07）。总体来看，研究揭示了尽管科学摘要正逐渐趋向战争隐喻，但军事化语言的使用可能削弱可信度、资金意愿和政策支持。

summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in long interactions, existing evaluations focus predominantly on retrospective memory (RM) via explicit queries. Prospective memory (PM), the critical ability to spontaneously recall and act on latent constraints without direct prompts, remains largely unevaluated. We introduce TriggerBench, a comprehensive PM benchmark spanning five dimensions across both daily assistants and professional workflows. TriggerBench pairs scenarios with matched RM controls, contrastive positive/negative variants, and overloaded triggers, enabling fine-grained measurement of proactive recall, false-alarm rate, and attentional robustness under a single protocol. Our evaluation yields three key findings. (i) PM shows a precision-recall trade-off and attentional fragility. Though enhanced reasoning significantly improves proactive recall, models may overfit to an "always-remind" heuristic. Furthermore, PM accuracy degrades substantially under implicit constraints or triggers overloaded by concurrent user requests, indicating that robust PM remains an open challenge. (ii) PM is notably harder than RM: on identical contexts, RM near-saturates up to 100K tokens, while PM decays sharply as context length scales. (iii) PM may serve as a behavioral probe of spare reasoning capacity. Pairing PM scenarios with AIME-2025 math problems reveals that successful trajectories yield higher PM accuracy than failed ones at the same context length, showing PM tracks spare reasoning budget that token count obscures. Project page: https://github.com/KristenZHANG/TriggerBench-Official.
title: TriggerBench: Investigating Prospective Memory for Large Language Models
title_translated: TriggerBench：探究大语言模型的前瞻性记忆
summary_translated: 随着大语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地应用于长时间交互，现有评估主要集中在通过显式查询进行的回溯性记忆（RM）测试。前瞻性记忆（PM），即在无直接提示下自发回忆并响应潜在约束的关键能力，仍基本未被评估。我们提出了TriggerBench，一个涵盖五个维度的综合性PM评估基准，覆盖日常助手和专业工作流程场景。TriggerBench通过匹配的RM对照、对比性的正负样本变体以及过载触发器设计，能够在单一协议下实现对主动回忆、误报率和注意力鲁棒性的细粒度测量。我们的评估得出三个关键发现：(i) PM存在精确率-召回率权衡和注意力脆弱性。虽然增强推理显著提升了主动回忆能力，但模型可能过度拟合于“始终提醒”的启发式策略。此外，当面临隐含约束或用户请求并发导致触发器过载时，PM准确率大幅下降，表明稳健的PM仍是开放挑战。(ii) PM明显比RM更难：在相同上下文长度下，RM在长达10万token时接近饱和，而PM随上下文增长迅速衰减。(iii) PM可作为剩余推理能力的行为探针。将PM场景与AIME-2025数学题配对后发现，成功轨迹在相同上下文长度下的PM准确率高于失败轨迹，说明PM能追踪token数量所掩盖的剩余推理资源。项目页面：https://github.com/KristenZHANG/TriggerBench-Official。

summary: Bias in natural language remains a persistent challenge in both human-written and AI-generated content, affecting domains such as journalism, education, and AI research. Most existing detection methods identify only the presence of bias, with limited support for granular detection, interpretable explanations, neutral rewriting, and openly available trained models. We present UnBias-Plus, an open-source toolkit unifying (1) segment-level multi-class bias classification, (2) biased span localization, (3) neutral text rewriting, and (4) reasoning for each decision. Available via Python, CLI, REST API, and web interfaces, UnBias-Plus supports accessible bias analysis. The toolkit, source code, models, datasets, and documentation are publicly available.
title: UnBias-Plus: Detect, Explain, and Rewrite Bias
title_translated: UnBias-Plus：检测、解释并重写偏见
summary_translated: 自然语言中的偏见问题在人类撰写和AI生成的内容中持续存在，影响新闻、教育和AI研究等多个领域。现有大多数检测方法仅能识别偏见的存在，缺乏对细粒度检测、可解释性说明、中立文本重写以及公开可用训练模型的支持。我们提出UnBias-Plus，一个开源工具包，整合了(1)段落级别的多类别偏见分类，(2)偏见片段定位，(3)中立文本重写，以及(4)每个决策的推理过程。该工具包通过Python、命令行、REST API和网页界面提供支持，便于进行偏见分析。工具包源代码、模型、数据集和文档均已公开可用。

summary: The emergence of Large Reasoning Models has introduced exceptionally long Chain-of-Thought traces, creating a transparency burden where critical logic is often buried under massive procedural text. To address this, we present ReasoningLens, an open-source framework designed for the hierarchical visualization and diagnostic auditing of complex reasoning chains. ReasoningLens addresses information necropsy by: (1) structuring traces into interactive hierarchies that separate high-level strategy from low-level execution; (2) leveraging an agentic auditor for automated error detection and tool-augmented verification; and (3) synthesizing systemic reasoning profiles to reveal model-specific blind spots. By transforming unstructured walls of text into actionable insights, ReasoningLens provides a modular foundation for interpreting, debugging, and optimizing the next generation of reasoning-centric AI.
title: ReasoningLens: Hierarchical Visualization and Diagnostic Auditing for Large Reasoning Models
comment: Our project is available at this https URL
title_translated: ReasoningLens：大推理模型的层次化可视化与诊断审计
summary_translated: 大推理模型的出现带来了极其冗长的思维链（Chain-of-Thought）痕迹，造成了透明性负担，关键逻辑往往深埋于大量程序性文本之下。为应对这一挑战，我们提出ReasoningLens，一个开源框架，旨在实现复杂推理链条的层次化可视化与诊断审计。ReasoningLens通过以下方式实现信息解剖：(1) 将推理痕迹结构化为可交互的层级，分离高层策略与底层执行；(2) 利用智能体审计员实现自动化错误检测与工具增强验证；(3) 合成系统性推理画像，揭示模型特有的盲点。通过将无结构的文本墙转化为可操作的洞察，ReasoningLens为解读、调试和优化下一代以推理为核心的AI提供了模块化基础。

summary: Pretrained text embeddings are increasingly used as representational maps, yet high category separability does not imply that their geometry recovers expert-defined structure. We study this problem in mental-health-related language, where symptom relations provide an external reference and online communities introduce strong domain, affective, stylistic, and discourse confounds. Using 28 Reddit communities, we compare pretrained and supervised fine-tuned Qwen3 embedding spaces at two scales (0.6B and 4B). We construct category prototypes, evaluate their representational dissimilarity matrices against an expert symptom matrix with representational similarity analysis, and complement this global test with prototype-based typicality and multi-baseline confound controls. Pretrained embeddings show measurable alignment with expert structure within the mental-health subset; fine-tuning strengthens this alignment most at the finest category level; and larger scale improves both zero-shot alignment and supervision-induced gains. Residual alignment remains substantial after controlling for VAD, LIWC, lexical style, and topic-distribution structure. These results suggest that LLM embeddings can recover expert-relevant category geometry, but this recovery is level-dependent and should be tested against explicit confounds rather than inferred from classification alone.
title: Do LLM Embedding Spaces Recover Expert Structure?
title_translated: LLM嵌入空间能否恢复专家结构？
summary_translated: 预训练文本嵌入正被越来越多地用作表征地图，但高类别可分性并不意味着其几何结构能恢复专家定义的结构。我们在与心理健康相关的语言中研究这一问题，其中症状关系提供了外部参考，而在线社区则引入了强烈的领域、情感、风格和话语混淆因素。我们使用28个Reddit社区，比较了0.6B和4B规模的预训练与监督微调后的Qwen3嵌入空间。我们构建了类别原型，通过表示相似性分析将它们的表示差异矩阵与专家症状矩阵进行对比，并结合基于原型的典型性及多基线混淆控制进行补充测试。结果显示，预训练嵌入在心理健康子集内与专家结构存在可测量的一致性；微调在最细粒度类别层级上显著增强了这种一致性；更大规模提升了零样本对齐效果和监督带来的增益。在控制VAD、LIWC、词汇风格和主题分布结构后，残余对齐依然显著。这些结果表明，LLM嵌入能够恢复与专家相关的类别几何结构，但这种恢复具有层级依赖性，应针对明确的混淆因素进行测试，而非仅从分类性能推断。

summary: Self-stigma predicts treatment avoidance and disengagement among people who use drugs (PWUD), yet conversational systems aiming to provide support typically treat self-stigma expression as a uniform signal. We present a three-phase, proof-of-concept study of a persona-aware approach to LLM support. Latent Profile Analysis (LPA) on indicator-level features from 1,174 self-stigma expressors on Reddit yields a four-persona typology validated against held-out behavioral and linguistic features. Sequential Bayesian and recurrent neural classifiers recover these personas from limited posting histories, substantially outperforming batch and few-shot LLM baselines (macro-F1 = 0.74 at 30 posts). Evaluation by eight clinical experts across three contemporary LLMs revealed a misalignment: persona-matched responses successfully achieved targeted behavioral shifts, yet raters holistically preferred the generic empathy of the persona-neutral baseline. Our findings suggest that holistic empathy judgments and clinically-aligned response design can pull in opposite directions, and that evaluating LLM-based stigma support requires rubrics capable of decomposing the two.
title: Self-Stigma Is Not a Monolith, but Generic Empathy Is: Persona-Conditioned LLM Support for People Who Use Drugs
title_translated: 自我污名并非单一整体，但普遍共情却是：面向药物使用者的人格化LLM支持
summary_translated: 自我污名会预测药物使用者（PWUD）回避治疗和脱离支持，然而旨在提供支持的对话系统通常将自我污名表达视为统一信号。我们提出了一项三阶段的概念验证研究，探讨一种人格感知的LLM支持方法。基于Reddit上1,174名自我污名表达者的指标级特征进行潜在剖面分析（LPA），得出一个四人格类型学，并通过保留的行为和语言特征进行了验证。序列贝叶斯和递归神经网络分类器从有限的发帖历史中成功恢复出这些人格，显著优于批量和少样本LLM基线（30条帖子时宏F1=0.74）。由八位临床专家对三种现代LLM进行评估发现存在偏差：人格匹配的回应虽能有效实现目标行为转变，但评分者整体更偏好无人格偏见基线所体现的普遍共情。我们的研究提示，整体共情判断与临床对齐的回应设计可能背道而驰，评估基于LLM的污名支持需采用能够分解这两者的评价标准。

summary: Transformer language models have become established tools for modeling human sentence processing, with measures such as surprisal and attention entropy serving as effective predictors of reading difficulty that together capture complementary aspects of processing load. Here, we explore a related class of transformer models: energy-based transformers, which provide a principled formal link to associative memory models, bringing processing research into direct contact with the broader literature on Hopfield networks and dense associative memory. To our knowledge, this is the first exploration of an energy-based transformer measure in computational psycholinguistics. Across reading-time corpora (Natural Stories, UCL eye-tracking, UCL self-paced reading), the energy measure is a robust predictor of reading times, providing significant fit beyond surprisal in all three. In a controlled experiment on relative clause processing, energy at a single layer captures the well-known object/subject asymmetry. We find evidence that it subsumes effects attributable to both attention entropy and surprisal, suggesting that energy may serve as a single unified predictor where multiple complementary measures have previously been required.
title: Energy-Based Transformers as Predictors of Reading Difficulty
title_translated: 基于能量的Transformer作为阅读难度的预测器
summary_translated: Transformer语言模型已成为建模人类句子处理的成熟工具，诸如意外度和注意力熵等度量能有效预测阅读难度，共同捕捉处理负荷的不同方面。本文探索了一类相关的Transformer模型：基于能量的Transformer，它为关联记忆模型提供了严谨的形式联系，使处理研究直接对接霍普菲尔德网络和密集关联记忆的广泛文献。据我们所知，这是首次在计算心理语言学中探索基于能量的Transformer度量。在多个阅读时间语料库（Natural Stories、UCL眼动追踪、UCL自控阅读）中，能量度量是阅读时间的稳健预测因子，在所有三个数据集中均显著超越意外度的拟合效果。在相对从句处理的受控实验中，单一层的能量值即可捕捉到众所周知的宾语/主语不对称现象。我们发现该能量度量可能涵盖了归因于注意力熵和意外度的效应，暗示能量或可作为单一统一预测器，替代此前所需的多种互补度量。

summary: While the wider applicability of LLMs in the legal field is currently debated due to their reliability and the gravity of any errors, narrow uses with well-understood and mitigated risks have emerged. Notably the Swiss Federal Supreme Court uses small on-premises models for tentative translations and short-passage summarization across the four official languages. However, such usage is challenging in the context of Criminal Law. Since rulings and cases employees work on routinely can contain detailed descriptions of violent and sexual offenses, their legitimate work is compromised by refusals and disclaimers due to the activation of model guardrails (over-alignment).   To measure this phenomenon, we introduce TF-RefusalBench, a multilingual benchmark for criminal-law translation and summarization derived from public Swiss Supreme Court rulings. TF-RefusalBench contains 5,200 total prompts across French, German, Italian, and English, corresponding to common task prompts and passages likely to trigger refusal. We then use TF-RefusalBench to show that over-alignment is a multifaceted phenomenon, influenced by the model and the prompt and text languages being processed, and that its impact cannot be evaluated solely from an over-refusal perspective, given the disclaimer's impact on task faithfulness. Finally, we evaluate approaches to enable on-premises LLMs for Criminal Law Tasks, demonstrating that while prompting can be effective, abliteration (refusal directions ablation) eliminates refusal with minimal impact on task performance.
title: Measuring & Mitigating Over-Alignment for LLMs in Multilingual Criminal Law Courts
title_translated: 多语言刑事法庭中LLM过度对齐的测量与缓解
summary_translated: 尽管LLM在法律领域的广泛应用目前仍存在争议，因其可靠性以及错误的严重性，但一些风险可控且已知的窄域应用已出现。例如瑞士联邦最高法院使用小型本地部署模型，对四种官方语言进行初步翻译和短段落摘要。然而，在刑事法背景下，此类应用面临挑战。由于判决和案件常包含暴力和性犯罪的详细描述，其合法工作因模型防护机制触发而被拒绝或附加免责声明（即过度对齐）。为衡量这一现象，我们提出了TF-RefusalBench，一个源自公开瑞士最高法院判决的多语言刑事法翻译与摘要基准。TF-RefusalBench包含5,200个跨法语、德语、意大利语和英语的提示，对应常见任务提示及可能引发拒绝的文本。我们利用该基准表明，过度对齐是多维度现象，受模型、提示及文本语言影响，其影响不能仅从过度拒绝角度评估，因为免责声明也会影响任务忠实度。最后，我们评估了使本地LLM适用于刑事法任务的方法，证明虽然提示工程有效，但消除拒绝指令（abliteration）可在最小影响任务表现的前提下彻底消除拒绝。

summary: As Large Language Models asymptotically approach human-level fluency in natural language generation, solely relying on surface-level semantic artifacts for detecting LLM-generated texts has become increasingly precarious. Existing detectors often falter when facing three critical challenges: adversarial perturbations, cross-domain shifts, and the rapid temporal evolution of the foundation model. To address these issues, we propose \wavedetect, a novel framework that reformulates text detection as a signal processing task within the time-frequency domain. Unlike previous methods that analyze static token probability distributions, \wavedetect models the generated output as a probability signal, upon which a differentiable Continuous Wavelet Transform is applied to convert them into learnable spectral representations. This process reveals the intrinsic ``spectral fingerprints'' in machine-generated texts--patterns that remain invisible in time domain. Comprehensive evaluations on three well-curated datasets (RAID, EvoBench, and Domain-Shift) show that our method achieves a new state-of-the-art. It not only achieves superior accuracy but also exhibits remarkable robustness against sophisticated attacks, generalization across out-of-distribution topics and unseen evolving LLMs. Our results validate the efficacy of spectral analysis as a promising paradigm for LLM-generated texts detection.
title: WaveDetect: Robust Framework for Machine-Generated Text Detection via Wavelet Transform
title_translated: WaveDetect：基于小波变换的鲁棒机器生成文本检测框架
summary_translated: 随着大型语言模型在自然语言生成中渐近逼近人类水平的流畅性，仅依赖表面语义特征来检测LLM生成文本已变得越来越不可靠。现有检测器在面对三大关键挑战时常出现失效：对抗性扰动、跨领域迁移以及基础模型的快速演进。为此，我们提出	exttt{WaveDetect}，一种将文本检测重新定义为时频域信号处理任务的新框架。不同于以往分析静态词元概率分布的方法，	exttt{WaveDetect}将生成输出建模为概率信号，并应用可微分的连续小波变换将其转化为可学习的谱表示。该过程揭示了机器生成文本中的内在“谱指纹”——这些模式在时域中不可见。在三个精心构建的数据集（RAID、EvoBench和Domain-Shift）上的全面评估显示，本方法达到新最优性能。不仅准确率领先，且对复杂攻击表现出卓越鲁棒性，具备跨分布话题和未见过的演化LLM的良好泛化能力。结果验证了谱分析作为LLM生成文本检测的有前景范式。

summary: Terminal-using agents have quickly become the most popular downstream application of language models (LMs). Despite their prevalence, relatively little academic work has examined RL-based training of these models, likely due to difficult benchmarks, a lack of data, and a lack of simple baseline recipes. We present Tmax, the strongest open RL recipe for terminal agents to date, bringing open data recipes closer to the frontier. While simple, our recipe achieves 27\% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 with only 9B parameters, outperforming much larger models from prior work. Concretely, we generate data using a novel taxonomy, combining difficulty control, personas, and verifier diversification, which allows us to cheaply generate large amounts of terminal environments for RL and SFT training. We open-source our terminal dataset, which is over 2.5x larger than previously released terminal-agent datasets. We then train open-weight models using RL with our data, using a simple, outcome-only recipe. We release our data, models, and code as a strong baseline for future open academic work on terminal agents at https://github.com/hamishivi/tmax.
title: Tmax: A simple recipe for terminal agents
title_translated: Tmax：终端智能体的简单训练方案
summary_translated: 使用终端的智能体已成为语言模型（LMs）最流行的下游应用。尽管应用广泛，但针对这些模型的基于强化学习（RL）的训练研究相对较少，可能是因为难以构建基准、缺乏数据以及缺少简单的基线方法。我们提出了Tmax，目前最强的开源强化学习训练方案，用于终端智能体，使开放数据方案更接近前沿水平。虽然方法简单，但仅用90亿参数就在Terminal-Bench 2.0上取得了27%的成绩，优于之前工作中的更大模型。具体而言，我们通过一种新颖的分类体系生成数据，结合难度控制、角色设定和验证器多样性，从而低成本生成大量适用于强化学习和监督微调（SFT）的终端环境。我们开源了终端数据集，其规模超过此前发布的终端智能体数据集的2.5倍。随后，我们使用该数据集对开放权重模型进行强化学习训练，采用一种简单的仅基于结果的训练方案。我们已将数据、模型和代码发布在https://github.com/hamishivi/tmax，作为未来开放学术研究中终端智能体的强基线。

summary: We study the limits of CTC-internal scoring for N-best hypothesis selection and locate the information bottleneck separating acoustic confidence from linguistic plausibility. Eleven CTC-internal and acoustic-feature scoring strategies produce no statistically significant WER improvement over greedy decoding on LibriSpeech dev-other at G=16 (all p > 0.05). The exhaustion is systematic: CTC's Spearman $ρ$ between hypothesis score and per-utterance WER degrades from -0.574 at G=4 to -0.270 at G=128, a 53% loss driven by blank-path proliferation. This establishes that the discriminative capacity of CTC-internal representations is saturated: no recombination of acoustic signals can close the oracle gap.   Confirming that the bottleneck is linguistic, not acoustic, external linguistic information introduced via MBR decoding breaks through it. MBR-CER decoding with a RoBERTa pseudo-log-likelihood (PLL) posterior ($τ$=10, G=128) achieves 5.42% WER on held-out LibriSpeech test-other (greedy 5.96%, $Δ$=-0.535 pp, p<0.0001, 9.0% relative). RoBERTa PLL $ρ$ degrades only 21% over the same range, retaining discriminating power where CTC loses it. Applied without retuning across two Zipformer architectures, three domains (LibriSpeech, TED-LIUM 3, VoxPopuli), and four MUSAN noise levels, the recipe gives significant gains in 11 of 13 conditions.   On the training side, standard MWER training via the CTC forward-backward algorithm implements Rao-Blackwellized REINFORCE at the output projection (variance about 3x below Viterbi). Yet sequence-level fine-tuning fails at near-converged checkpoints: all four MWER configurations on CR-CTC collapse (+6.18 to +8.90 pp WER), as a training oracle gap of 0.007 pp provides no usable reward signal.
title: The Anatomy of the CTC Oracle Gap: Acoustic Exhaustion and Linguistic Recovery
comment: 30 pages, 8 figures. Code and data: this https URL
title_translated: CTC 优化器差距的剖析：声学耗尽与语言恢复
summary_translated: 我们研究了CTC内部评分在N-best候选选择中的极限，并定位了声学置信度与语言合理性之间的信息瓶颈。在LibriSpeech dev-other数据集上，G=16时，十一种CTC内部及声学特征评分策略均未显著改善贪婪解码的WER（所有p > 0.05）。这种耗尽是系统性的：CTC的假设得分与每句话WER之间的Spearman相关系数从G=4时的-0.574下降到G=128时的-0.270，损失达53%，主要由空白路径泛滥导致。这表明CTC内部表示的判别能力已饱和：任何声学信号的重新组合都无法弥合最优解差距。确认瓶颈在于语言而非声学后，通过MBR解码引入外部语言信息可突破该瓶颈。使用RoBERTa伪对数似然（PLL）后验（τ=10, G=128）的MBR-CER解码在LibriSpeech test-other上达到5.42% WER（贪婪解码为5.96%，Δ=-0.535 pp，p<0.0001，相对提升9.0%）。RoBERTa PLL的相关系数在同一范围内仅下降21%，保留了CTC丧失的判别能力。该方法在不重新调参的情况下，应用于两个Zipformer架构、三个领域（LibriSpeech、TED-LIUM 3、VoxPopuli）和四种MUSAN噪声水平，在13种条件中有11种取得显著提升。在训练方面，通过CTC前向-后向算法实现的标准MWER训练在输出投影层实现了Rao-Blackwellized REINFORCE（方差约为Viterbi的三分之一）。然而，序列级微调在接近收敛的检查点处失败：CR-CTC上的四种MWER配置全部崩溃（WER上升+6.18至+8.90 pp），因为训练最优解差距仅为0.007 pp，无法提供可用的奖励信号。

summary: Generative Spoken Language Modeling (GSLM) enables text-free speech modeling by training language models (LMs) using discrete speech representations instead of textual transcription. In this paper, we investigate the performance of GSLM on speech synthesis and continuation using discrete speech representations with varying bitrates. We segment speech representations with fixed widths and train K-means models in multiple cluster sizes, resulting in various bitrate settings. We demonstrate that intelligible and natural speech can be synthesized at lower bitrate settings than the baseline. Furthermore, speech continuation quality remains stable at lower bitrates across multiple metrics, suggesting that the conventional GSLM setting may be redundant for effective speech generation. Although LLM-based metrics show higher correlation with human subjective score than conventional metrics, it remains low, highlighting the need for more stable automatic evaluation methods.
title: On the Effect of Segmentation Width and Cluster Size on Speech Resynthesis and Continuation in Generative Spoken Language Models
title_translated: 分段宽度与聚类大小对生成式口语语言模型语音重合成与延续的影响
summary_translated: 生成式口语语言建模（GSLM）通过使用离散语音表示而非文本转录来实现无文本语音建模。本文研究了使用不同比特率的离散语音表示在语音合成与延续任务中的表现。我们采用固定宽度对语音表示进行分段，并在多种聚类大小下训练K-means模型，从而形成不同的比特率设置。结果表明，在低于基线比特率的情况下仍可合成清晰自然的语音。此外，多个指标显示，较低比特率下的语音延续质量保持稳定，提示传统GSLM设置对于有效语音生成可能是冗余的。尽管基于大语言模型（LLM）的评估指标比传统指标与人类主观评分的相关性更高，但相关性仍然偏低，凸显了需要更稳定的自动评估方法。

summary: Memory systems are essential for personalized Large Language Models (LLMs). However, existing retrieval methods in these systems primarily rely on semantic similarity, potentially missing logically critical memories with limited semantic overlap. Current benchmarks remain inadequate for evaluating this problem. To address this gap, we construct IMLogic, the first high-quality benchmark targeting implicit logical memory retrieval in long-dialogue scenarios. Motivated by this challenge, we introduce root memory, a structured, decision-preserving representation that distills reusable personalized logic from long-term user histories. We then propose RootMem, a plug-and-play framework that first distills raw histories into structured root memories and then uses an LLM-based router to activate logically relevant ones, complementing semantic retrieval with personalized decision logic. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RootMem significantly outperforms the strongest retrieval baselines and consistently boosts the accuracy of existing memory agents. Our benchmark and codes will be available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/IMLogic-DBB3.
title: Towards Root Memories: Benchmarking and Enhancing Implicit Logical Memory Retrieval for Personalized LLMs
title_translated: 迈向根记忆：面向个性化大语言模型的隐式逻辑记忆检索的基准测试与增强
summary_translated: 记忆系统对个性化大语言模型（LLMs）至关重要。然而，现有检索方法主要依赖语义相似性，可能遗漏语义重叠有限但逻辑上关键的记忆。当前的基准测试仍不足以评估此类问题。为填补这一空白，我们构建了IMLogic，首个高质量的基准测试，专注于长对话场景中的隐式逻辑记忆检索。受此挑战启发，我们提出“根记忆”——一种结构化、决策保留的表示形式，能够从长期用户历史中提炼出可复用的个性化逻辑。随后，我们提出RootMem，一个即插即用的框架：首先将原始历史转化为结构化的根记忆，再利用基于LLM的路由器激活逻辑相关的记忆，从而在语义检索之外补充个性化决策逻辑。大量实验表明，RootMem显著优于最强的检索基线，并持续提升现有记忆代理的准确性。我们的基准测试和代码将公开于https://anonymous.4open.science/r/IMLogic-DBB3。

summary: Knowledge injection via synthetic data is crucial for enhancing Large Language Models (LLMs). However, current synthesis methods simply stop at preset token counts or fixed data ratios, lacking awareness of knowledge distribution. This results in some domains being sparse while others are redundant, limiting LLM knowledge boundaries. We revisit knowledge injection from a distribution perspective and hypothesize that an optimal knowledge distribution exists to maximize knowledge boundary expansion. We propose KDoS (Knowledge Distribution-optimized Synthesis), a framework that introduces knowledge density to drive synthesis through a three-stage feedback mechanism, shifting from blind generation to distribution-optimized synthesis. We construct Wikipedia-based synthetic data with varying knowledge distributions and conduct experiments on models from 0.6B to 16B (Qwen, Ling, LLaMA) and data scales from 1B to 5B tokens. Our key findings are: (1) an optimal knowledge distribution consistently maximizes boundary expansion; (2) this distribution is stable across backbones and scales; (3) KDoS outperforms baselines across six knowledge benchmarks. Our work offers a new perspective and practical framework for synthetic data-driven knowledge injection.
title: Scaling LLM Knowledge Boundaries via Distribution-Optimized Synthesis
title_translated: 通过分布优化合成扩展大语言模型的知识边界
summary_translated: 通过合成数据注入知识对提升大语言模型（LLMs）至关重要。然而，当前合成方法通常仅在预设的词元数量或固定数据比例处停止，缺乏对知识分布的认知。这导致某些领域知识稀疏而其他领域冗余，限制了LLM知识边界的扩展。我们从分布视角重新审视知识注入，并假设存在一种最优知识分布以最大化知识边界的扩展。为此，我们提出KDoS（知识分布优化合成）框架，引入知识密度并通过三阶段反馈机制驱动合成，实现从盲目生成到分布优化合成的转变。我们构建了基于维基百科的合成数据，涵盖不同知识分布，并在0.6B至16B（Qwen、Ling、LLaMA）的模型以及1B至5B词元的数据规模上进行了实验。主要发现包括：（1）最优知识分布能持续最大化边界扩展；（2）该分布跨模型架构和数据规模具有稳定性；（3）KDoS在六个知识基准测试中均优于基线方法。本工作为基于合成数据的知识注入提供了新视角和实用框架。

summary: Automatic review generation is a promising direction for accelerating scientific progress. While most work adopts an end-to-end setup, its fully automated nature makes it less suitable for settings that demand accountability. To better balance automation and accountability, we formalize judgment-grounded expansion, a human-AI collaboration mode where a reviewer provides an evaluative claim and the system expands it into review comment candidate(s). We model it as a structured generate-check-refine process and conduct a user study to collect human-model interaction data. We study two practical challenges for judgment-grounded expansion: scalable evaluation and candidate set curation. We develop methods to simulate the process for large-scale evaluation, and show that conformal prediction is well suited to balancing candidate set size and target coverage. Our work establishes judgment-grounded expansion as a concrete task and provides empirical and methodological foundations for the design of future collaborative review generation systems.
title: Judgment-Grounded Expansion for Peer Review Generation
title_translated: 基于判断的扩展用于同行评审生成
summary_translated: 自动评审生成是加速科学进步的一个有前景的方向。尽管大多数研究采用端到端设置，但其完全自动化的特点使其在需要问责制的场景中不太适用。为更好地平衡自动化与问责性，我们提出了基于判断的扩展，这是一种人机协作模式：评审员提供一个评估性陈述，系统则将其扩展为评审意见候选。我们将该过程建模为结构化的生成-检查-优化流程，并通过用户研究收集了人机交互数据。我们研究了基于判断扩展中的两个实际挑战：可扩展评估和候选集整理。我们开发了方法来模拟大规模评估过程，并表明置信预测非常适合在候选集大小和目标覆盖率之间取得平衡。我们的工作将基于判断的扩展确立为一个具体任务，并为未来协同评审生成系统的设计提供了实证和方法论基础。

summary: LLM agents are increasingly deployed in multi-party environments, handling sensitive personal data on behalf of individual users, for instance in group chats. When such an agent discloses private information, it reaches every group member at once. This risk is structurally harder to control than in one-to-one settings, as every piece of private information must be appropriate for every recipient in the group. Yet all existing contextual privacy benchmarks consider only single-interlocutor settings, leaving multi-party privacy risks unmeasured. We introduce MuPPET (Multi-Party Privacy Exposure Testing), a benchmark for contextual privacy in multi-party conversations. Our experiments show that models leak substantially more in multi-party settings than one-to-one evaluations suggest. Frontier models are vulnerable, and smaller open-weights models, often preferred for local deployment with sensitive data, even more so. Existing contextual privacy defences offer only partial protection, degrade utility, and do not resolve the underlying party-tracking problem.
title: MuPPET: A Benchmark for Contextual Privacy of LLM Assistants in Multi-Party Conversations
title_translated: MuPPET：多参与者对话中LLM助手上下文隐私的基准测试
summary_translated: 大型语言模型代理越来越多地被部署在多参与者环境中，代表单个用户处理敏感个人数据，例如在群聊中。当此类代理泄露私人信息时，所有群组成员会同时收到。这种风险在结构上比一对一场景更难控制，因为每条私人信息都必须对群组中的每个接收者都合适。然而，现有的上下文隐私基准测试仅考虑单对话方场景，导致多参与者隐私风险未被衡量。我们引入了MuPPET（多参与者隐私暴露测试），用于多参与者对话中上下文隐私的基准测试。我们的实验表明，模型在多参与者场景下的泄露程度远高于一对一评估所暗示的程度。前沿模型存在脆弱性，而较小的开源权重模型（通常因其本地部署敏感数据而受青睐）甚至更为脆弱。现有的上下文隐私防御措施仅提供部分保护，降低实用性，并未能解决根本的参与者追踪问题。

summary: Intrinsic self-correction (SC) aims to improve large language model outputs by prompting a model to revisit its own initial answer without external feedback. Recent studies have questioned the reliability of this approach, showing that models often struggle to judge whether their initial responses are correct. In this work, we take a task-sensitive view of SC. Rather than asking whether it works in general, we examine settings where SC may operate through different mechanisms: verifying explicit constraints, revisiting a complex reasoning process, or providing a second opinion over competing strategies in word-game tasks. Across multiple benchmarks and models, we find that SC can yield consistent performance gains when the underlying task structure facilitates these modes of revision. These results suggest that SC is best understood as a task-dependent inference-time strategy whose usefulness depends on the role the revision stage can play in a given task, rather than as a uniformly reliable method for improving initial model outputs.
title: When Does Intrinsic Self-Correction Help? A Task-Sensitive Analysis
title_translated: 内在自我修正何时有效？一项任务敏感性分析
summary_translated: 内在自我修正（SC）旨在通过提示大语言模型重新审视其初始回答而不依赖外部反馈来改进输出。近期研究质疑该方法的可靠性，指出模型常常难以判断其初始回答是否正确。在本工作中，我们从任务敏感的角度看待SC。不问其是否普遍有效，而是考察SC可能通过不同机制发挥作用的情境：验证显式约束、重新审视复杂推理过程，或在词语游戏任务中对竞争策略提供第二意见。在多个基准测试和模型上的实验表明，当任务结构支持这些修订模式时，SC能够带来一致的性能提升。结果表明，SC最好被理解为一种依赖任务的推理时策略，其有效性取决于修订阶段在特定任务中所能发挥的作用，而非一种普遍可靠的改进初始输出的方法。

summary: Who invented the radio, Russia's Alexander Popov or Italy's Guglielmo Marconi? Was the telephone the achievement of Bell in the United States or Meucci in Italy? Does printing belong to China's Bi Sheng or Germany's Gutenberg? The answer depends not only on historical record but also on language and perspective.   We analyse eleven widely used large language models across 21 disputed inventions and discoveries, evaluated in twelve languages and 75,896 responses. While models generally acknowledge that credit is contested, query language systematically affects which claimant is surfaced. Lower-status claimants are more likely to appear when questions are asked in their associated language, whereas dominant Anglophone figures remain stable across languages.   These patterns persist after controlling for response length, model differences, historical prominence, and levels of national commemoration. Language thus acts as a switch that activates different national versions of the same history, producing systematically different national memories from the same question.   We interpret this as evidence that large language models function as distributed systems of cultural memory, where language conditions which histories become visible, contributing to a computational form of banal nationalism.
title: Same question, different history: language, national identity, and credit in large language models
comment: 27 pages (main text and Supplementary Information combined), 5 figures, 9 tables
title_translated: 同一问题，不同历史：语言、民族认同与大语言模型中的功劳归属
summary_translated: 谁发明了无线电，是俄罗斯的波波夫还是意大利的马可尼？电话是贝尔在美国的成就，还是梅乌奇在意大利的贡献？印刷术属于中国的毕昇还是德国的古腾堡？答案不仅取决于历史记录，还取决于语言和视角。我们分析了11个广泛使用的大型语言模型在21个有争议的发明与发现上的表现，涵盖12种语言和75,896次响应。尽管模型普遍承认功劳存在争议，但提问语言系统性地影响了哪一方被突出呈现。使用相关语言提问时，地位较低的主张者更有可能出现，而主导性的英语人物则在各种语言中保持稳定。这些模式在控制响应长度、模型差异、历史显著性和国家纪念水平后依然存在。因此，语言如同开关，激活同一历史的不同民族版本，使相同问题产生系统性不同的民族记忆。我们认为这表明大型语言模型作为文化记忆的分布式系统，语言决定了哪些历史变得可见，从而促成一种计算形式的日常民族主义。

summary: Large language models have demonstrated significant capabilities in generating diverse and context-aware responses for empathetic dialogue. However, their computational demands severely limit their deployment in resource-constrained environments. While knowledge distillation offers a promising compression solution, it often fails to transfer the nuanced understanding essential for empathy, as it overlooks the implicit contextual cues that guide human connection. To bridge this gap, we propose a \textbf{pr}ivileged \textbf{i}nformation-enhanced knowledge \textbf{d}istillation method for \textbf{e}mpathetic dialogue generation (PRIDE). Our method leverages privileged information, such as expert psychological annotations or future event summaries, which is available exclusively during training but unavailable at inference time. This allows us to transfer the teacher model's empathetic reasoning to smaller models without relying on extra inputs during deployment. Specifically, PRIDE has three key components: (1) An empathy-reasoning prompt that guides the teacher to explicitly decompose the empathetic process into understanding feelings and analyzing situations step-by-step; (2) A multi-source attention mechanism that directs the student to effectively integrate privileged information; (3) A dual-alignment loss that combines reversed Kullback-Leibler divergence and maximum mean discrepancy to ensure robust knowledge transfer at both logit and feature levels. Experiments on multi-modal and text-only datasets demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance, and in some cases matches or even surpasses larger teacher models in terms of accuracy and semantic relevance.
title: PRIDE: Privileged Information-enhanced Distillation for Empathetic Dialogue Generation
title_translated: PRIDE：增强特权信息的知识蒸馏用于共情对话生成
summary_translated: 大型语言模型在生成多样化且情境感知的共情对话回复方面表现出显著能力。然而，其计算开销严重限制了其在资源受限环境中的部署。虽然知识蒸馏提供了一种有前景的压缩解决方案，但它往往无法传递情感理解所需的细微差别，因为它忽略了引导人类连接的隐含上下文线索。为弥合这一差距，我们提出了一种面向共情对话生成的	extbf{pr}ivileged 	extbf{i}nformation-enhanced knowledge 	extbf{d}istillation方法（PRIDE）。该方法利用训练期间可用但在推理时不可用的特权信息（如专家心理标注或未来事件摘要），从而在不依赖部署时额外输入的情况下，将教师模型的情感推理能力转移到小型模型。具体而言，PRIDE包含三个关键组件：(1) 情感推理提示，引导教师逐步分解情感过程，包括理解感受和分析情境；(2) 多源注意力机制，指导学生有效整合特权信息；(3) 双重对齐损失，结合反向KL散度与最大均值差异，确保在logit和特征层面实现稳健的知识迁移。在多模态和纯文本数据集上的实验表明，该方法取得了具有竞争力的表现，某些情况下甚至在准确性和语义相关性上达到或超过更大的教师模型。

summary: With the increasing demands for advanced document conversion, mapping structured Markdown drafts into template-compliant formats like LaTeX remains a challenge. Existing approaches largely depend on either deterministic rule-based converters or pure end-to-end Large Language Model (LLM) generation. The former fails to correctly handle asset insertions and template-specific constraints, while the latter tends to induce semantic drift, leading to hallucinations that are difficult to debug. To address these limitations, we introduce a robust Dual-Track Framework that systematically decouples template formatting from document processing: an offline track extracts template constraints into a reusable manifest, while an online track implements a hybrid execution pipeline. This pipeline confines LLM usage exclusively to reasoning-intensive components (e.g., semantic metadata, bibliographic references, and complex visual/tabular layouts) while delegating rule-based engines for deterministic processing. Empirical evaluation across 7 LaTeX templates and 56 published research papers demonstrates that our method preserves better structural fidelity, satisfies diverse layout constraints, and achieves a higher compilation success rate compared to the previous baselines.
title: A Dual-Track Framework for Template-Constrained LaTeX Conversion
title_translated: 一种用于模板约束型LaTeX转换的双轨框架
summary_translated: 随着对高级文档转换需求的增长，将结构化Markdown草稿映射为符合模板要求的LaTeX格式仍是一项挑战。现有方法主要依赖于确定性的规则驱动转换器或纯端到端的大语言模型（LLM）生成。前者无法正确处理资产插入和模板特定约束，而后者容易引发语义漂移，导致难以调试的幻觉问题。为解决这些局限性，我们提出了一种稳健的双轨框架，系统性地将模板格式化与文档处理解耦：离线轨道将模板约束提取为可重用的清单，而在线轨道则实现混合执行流程。该流程仅在需要推理的组件（如语义元数据、参考文献和复杂的视觉/表格布局）中使用LLM，而将确定性处理任务交由基于规则的引擎完成。在7个LaTeX模板和56篇已发表研究论文上的实证评估表明，我们的方法在保持更好结构保真度、满足多样化布局约束以及实现更高编译成功率方面优于先前基线。

summary: Humans possess an innate ability to understand fine-grained interpersonal relationships, which is central to everyday social interactions. Although such reasoning is inherently multimodal, it remains largely unexplored by existing multimodal large language models (MLLMs). To address this gap, we introduce PIVOTS, the first benchmark built from Social-IQ 2.0 and YouTube data to evaluate MLLMs' ability to predict bidirectional interpersonal relationship dimensions grounded in established psychology research. In addition, PIVOTS includes auxiliary tasks that assess models' ability to identify and leverage the critical visual cues underlying such predictions. We evaluate both proprietary and open-source MLLMs and conduct detailed ablation studies to analyze the effects of visual modalities and explicit social role information in conversational utterances. We further examine how joint and pairwise prediction settings benefit MLLMs in scoring bidirectional PIVOTS dimensions. Project page and resources: https://flynnzhangsx.github.io/PIVOTSBench/ .
title: PIVOTSBench: Evaluating Fine-Grained Interpersonal Relationship Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models
title_translated: PIVOTSBench：评估多模态大语言模型中的细粒度人际关系推理能力
summary_translated: 人类天生具备理解细粒度人际关系的能力，这是日常社交互动的核心。尽管这种推理本质上是多模态的，但现有的多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）对此仍缺乏深入探索。为填补这一空白，我们提出了PIVOTS，首个基于Social-IQ 2.0和YouTube数据构建的基准，用于评估MLLMs预测双向人际关系维度的能力，其依据来自心理学研究。此外，PIVOTS还包含辅助任务，以评估模型识别并利用支撑此类预测的关键视觉线索的能力。我们评估了专有和开源的MLLMs，并进行了详细的消融实验，分析视觉模态和对话中显式社会角色信息的影响。我们进一步考察了联合与成对预测设置如何提升MLLMs在评分双向PIVOTS维度方面的表现。项目页面及资源：https://flynnzhangsx.github.io/PIVOTSBench/。

summary: Phones are becoming an important execution surface for general-purpose agents, but training open models for reliable phone use remains difficult because the environment that matters at deployment, real devices running real apps, is slow, stateful, side-effectful, and hard to reset or verify, while scalable mock environments only approximate real behavior. We present PhoneBuddy, a training recipe and open-model line for agentic phone use that combines a real-app environment with a mock-app environment, PhoneWorld, which reconstructs runnable mock apps from real GUI usage structure. PhoneBuddy first builds a shared supervised fine-tuning stage from trajectories collected in both environments, then compares real-app RL against mixed RL across both environments. Across a 150-task human evaluation on real phones spanning apps, mini-apps, and cross-app workflows, task success rate improves from 36.67\% after supervised fine-tuning to 40.67\% after real-app RL and 45.33\% after mixed RL. On AndroidWorld, the same progression rises from 60.3\% to 77.2\% to 83.2\%. These results show that mock-app training is not a replacement for real-app RL, but a complementary source of scalable, resettable, and automatically checked interaction. The gains are strongest on app and mini-app tasks, while long-horizontal cross-app workflows remain an important open challenge.
title: Training Open Models for Agentic Phone Use
title_translated: 面向代理式手机使用的开放模型训练
summary_translated: 手机正成为通用代理的重要执行界面，但训练开放模型以实现可靠的手机使用仍具挑战性，因为部署时所依赖的真实设备运行真实应用的环境具有响应慢、状态持久、存在副作用且难以重置或验证的特点，而可扩展的模拟环境仅能近似真实行为。我们提出了PhoneBuddy，一种用于代理式手机使用的训练方案和开放模型路径，结合真实应用环境与模拟应用环境PhoneWorld——后者从真实GUI使用结构重建可运行的模拟应用。PhoneBuddy首先在两个环境中收集轨迹，建立共享的监督微调阶段，然后比较真实应用强化学习（RL）与跨两种环境的混合RL。在涵盖应用、小程序和跨应用工作流的150项真实手机任务的人类评估中，任务成功率从监督微调后的36.67%提升至真实应用RL后的40.67%，再提升至混合RL后的45.33%。在AndroidWorld中，同一进展从60.3%升至77.2%再到83.2%。结果表明，模拟应用训练并非真实应用RL的替代品，而是可扩展、可重置且自动验证交互的互补来源。收益在应用和小程序任务上最为显著，而长横向跨应用工作流仍是重要开放挑战。

summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) raise growing concerns about privacy leakage and copyright compliance. Membership inference is a key tool for assessing such risks, but existing studies mainly focus on whether specific samples or sample-based data units are used for training. We argue that LLMs exhibit a human-memory-like behavior: an LLM may not memorize a specific sample verbatim, yet it can accumulate and reveal knowledge about a real-world entity from scattered mentions. This analogy motivates us to examine whether an LLM can be interrogated like a human interviewee to reveal its exposure to entity-related information. Motivated by this question, we propose entity-level membership inference, which determines whether information related to a target entity is used in LLM training. We study this task in the practical label-only black-box setting, where only generated texts are observable. We formalize the task under clue, input, and model constraints, establish the necessary and sufficient conditions for its feasibility, and instantiate five interrogation strategies based on this formalization. The strategies use limited entity clues to construct prompts, elicit entity-related responses, and infer membership from semantic features among the generated texts. We construct entity-level datasets and adapt state-of-the-art sample-level label-only methods to the entity-level setting as baselines. Experiments on person entities show that our methods achieve AUC up to 0.97 and bring gains of 6.0%--17.5% in Balanced Accuracy over the best adapted baseline.
title: Have You Ever Seen Them? Entity-level Membership Inference through Interrogating Large Language Models
title_translated: 你见过他们吗？通过质询大语言模型进行实体级成员身份推断
summary_translated: 大语言模型（LLMs）引发了关于隐私泄露和版权合规性的日益增长的担忧。成员身份推断是评估此类风险的关键工具，但现有研究主要关注特定样本或基于样本的数据单元是否被用于训练。我们认为，LLMs表现出类似人类记忆的行为：LLM可能不会逐字记忆某个具体样本，但可以从零散提及中积累并揭示关于现实世界实体的知识。这一类比促使我们思考：能否像质询人类受访者一样，通过质询来揭示LLM对实体相关资讯的暴露情况。受此启发，我们提出了实体级成员身份推断，旨在判断目标实体的相关信息是否被用于LLM训练。我们在仅可观测生成文本的实用标签仅黑盒设置下研究该任务。我们在线索、输入和模型约束下形式化该任务，确立其可行性的必要与充分条件，并基于此形式化设计了五种质询策略。这些策略利用有限的实体线索构造提示，引出与实体相关的回应，并从生成文本间的语义特征中推断成员身份。我们构建了实体级数据集，并将最先进的样本级标签仅方法适配到实体级设置作为基线。针对人物实体的实验表明，我们的方法AUC最高达0.97，在平衡准确率上相比最佳适配基线提升6.0%–17.5%。

summary: This article aims to evaluate the quality of machine translation (MT) and post-editing (PE) in the context of specialised translation from English into French. Three MT systems (DeepL, eTranslation and Systran) were compared, and two groups of post-editors -linguists/translators and NLP experts -were asked to perform post-editing. Translation assessment is based on error annotation using an error typology adapted to MT and PE evaluation. The results reveal significant differences between the three MT systems and the two groups of post-editors, particularly in terms of terminological accuracy and fluency. This study highlights the importance of domain knowledge in specialised translation, as well as the limitations and variable performance of MT systems in language for specific purposes (LSP).
title: Machine Translation and Post-Editing: Comparative Evaluation of Different MT Systems and Post-Editor Groups in Specialised Translation
title_translated: 机器翻译与后期编辑：不同MT系统与编辑者群体在专业翻译中的对比评估
summary_translated: 本文旨在评估专业翻译场景下从英语到法语的机器翻译（MT）与后期编辑（PE）的质量。比较了三种MT系统（DeepL、eTranslation和Systran），并让两组后期编辑者——语言学家/翻译人员与NLP专家——执行编辑任务。翻译评估基于针对MT和PE评估调整的错误类型学进行错误标注。结果揭示了三种MT系统与两组编辑者之间存在显著差异，特别是在术语准确性和流畅性方面。本研究强调了专业翻译中领域知识的重要性，以及MT系统在特定用途语言（LSP）中的局限性和性能波动。

summary: Knowledge graphs (KGs) are increasingly used as structured context for Large Language Models (LLMs), but industrial KG-RAG systems often need to integrate public and domain-specific KGs constructed from heterogeneous databases. This integration relies on Entity Alignment (EA), where lexical matching alone is insufficient under predicate-name variation and incomplete local neighborhoods. We address EA for KG integration by constructing a pairwise EA dataset and proposing two complementary modules: Predicate Importance Estimation (PIE) and Decoupled Rationale-Score Distillation (DRSD). PIE is a compact embedding-based approach that removes the subject information from each 1-hop triple, encodes the resulting subjectless triples, and aggregates them with learnable predicate-importance weights to build predicate-aware entity embeddings. DRSD trains a distilled small language model (SLM) with pseudo-answers produced by a teacher LLM through distinct prompts. By converting binary EA labels into text-based supervision and decoupling confidence-score estimation from label-consistent rationales, DRSD enables the SLM to learn task-specific reasoning while retaining a less label-biased confidence signal. Experiments show that PIE and DRSD improve EA classification. Moreover, because DRSD decouples confidence-score estimation from the decision, a discrepancy between the two flags an uncertain prediction for human review, thereby enabling a practical discrepancy between automatic acceptance and human-in-the-loop verification.
title: Predicate Importance Estimation and Decoupled Rationale-Score Distillation for Entity Alignment
title_translated: 谓词重要性估计与解耦推理-得分蒸馏用于实体对齐
summary_translated: 知识图谱（KG）越来越多地被用作大型语言模型（LLM）的结构化上下文，但工业级KG-RAG系统通常需要整合来自异构数据库构建的公共和领域特定知识图谱。这种整合依赖于实体对齐（EA），在谓词名称变化和局部邻域不完整的情况下，仅靠词汇匹配是不够的。我们通过构建成对EA数据集并提出两个互补模块来解决KG集成中的EA问题：谓词重要性估计（PIE）和解耦推理-得分蒸馏（DRSD）。PIE是一种紧凑的基于嵌入的方法，它从每个一跳三元组中移除主体信息，编码得到的无主体三元组，并使用可学习的谓词重要性权重进行聚合，以构建具备谓词感知能力的实体嵌入。DRSD通过教师LLM生成的伪答案，利用不同提示训练一个蒸馏的小语言模型（SLM）。通过将二分类EA标签转换为基于文本的监督信号，并将置信度得分估计与标签一致的推理解耦，DRSD使SLM能够学习任务特定的推理，同时保留较少受标签偏差影响的置信信号。实验表明，PIE和DRSD提升了EA分类性能。此外，由于DRSD将置信度得分估计与决策解耦，两者之间的差异可标记为不确定预测以供人工审查，从而实现自动接受与人工介入验证之间的实际差异。

summary: Statistical analysis is a broad, complex field requiring both domain knowledge and tool proficiency. While prior work has evaluated large language models (LLMs) in this domain, existing benchmarks remain limited in scope and format. To bridge this gap, we introduce StatABench (Statistical AnalysisBenchmark), a benchmark designed to systematically assess LLMs' statistical analysis capabilities. StatABench comprises two complementary components: Stat-Closed, containing 404 questions across 18 statistical topics in multiple formats (multiple-choice, fill-in-the-blank, decision-making, and practical application), and Stat-Open, featuring 30 complex open-ended modeling tasks adapted from professional competitions. We evaluate diverse LLMs using the LangChain MCP framework and multiple data science agents, and assess Stat-Open solutions via a validated LLM-as-Judge protocol. Experiments show that even GPT-5.1 achieves only 68.6% on Stat-Closed, while the best open-source model reaches 60.6%. On Stat-Open, the top agent framework scores 61.86 on average. These results reveal the gap between current LLMs and reliable statistical analysis, highlighting persistent challenges in tool-grounded reasoning, methodological decision-making, and end-to-end statistical modeling.
title: StatABench: Dataset and Framework for Evaluating Statistical Analysis Capabilities of LLMs
title_translated: StatABench：评估大语言模型统计分析能力的数据集与框架
summary_translated: 统计分析是一个广泛而复杂的领域，既需要领域知识又要求工具熟练度。尽管已有研究评估过大语言模型（LLM）在该领域的表现，但现有基准在范围和格式上仍存在局限。为弥补这一差距，我们提出了StatABench（统计分析基准），一个旨在系统评估LLM统计分析能力的基准。StatABench包含两个互补组件：Stat-Closed包含404个问题，覆盖18个统计主题，采用多种格式（选择题、填空题、决策题和实际应用题）；Stat-Open则包含30个复杂开放式的建模任务，改编自专业竞赛。我们使用LangChain MCP框架和多个数据科学代理评估多种LLM，并通过经验证的LLM作为裁判协议评估Stat-Open的解决方案。实验显示，即使GPT-5.1在Stat-Closed上也仅达到68.6%的准确率，而最佳开源模型达到60.6%。在Stat-Open上，顶级代理框架平均得分为61.86。这些结果揭示了当前LLM与可靠统计分析之间的差距，突显了工具导向推理、方法决策以及端到端统计建模方面的持续挑战。

summary: Large language models (LLMs) achieve strong performance across many tasks, but their high computational cost limits deployment in resource-constrained environments. Knowledge Distillation (KD) offers a practical solution by transferring knowledge from a teacher model of a larger size to a smaller student model. While prior work has mainly examined task-specific or small-scale settings, the post-training stage for building general instruction-following models has received limited attention. In this paper, we conduct a systematic study of KD in post-training using the large-scale Tulu 3 dataset. We find that KD outperforms supervised fine-tuning (SFT) in low-data regimes, but its advantage diminishes as more training data is added. Distilling from a stronger instruction-tuned teacher restores substantial gains even with abundant data, indicating that KD remains effective when the teacher provides knowledge that the student cannot easily acquire from the training data alone. We further study domain-specific, low-resource scenarios and propose a two-stage KD strategy that leverages synthetic teacher-labeled data followed by refinement on human annotations. This method consistently improves student performance, providing practical guidance for building compact models in data-scarce environments.
title: Understanding Knowledge Distillation in Post-Training: When It Helps and When It Fails
title_translated: 理解后训练阶段的知识蒸馏：何时有效，何时失效
summary_translated: 大型语言模型（LLM）在众多任务中表现出强大性能，但其高计算成本限制了在资源受限环境中的部署。知识蒸馏（KD）提供了一种实用解决方案，即将大尺寸教师模型的知识转移到较小的学生模型中。尽管先前研究主要集中在特定任务或小规模设置，但针对构建通用指令遵循模型的后训练阶段关注较少。本文通过对大规模Tulu 3数据集进行系统研究，探讨了后训练阶段的知识蒸馏。我们发现，在低数据环境下，KD优于监督微调（SFT），但随着训练数据量增加，其优势逐渐减弱。从更强的指令微调教师模型中蒸馏知识，即使在数据充足时也能恢复显著收益，表明当教师模型提供了学生模型无法仅从训练数据中轻易获取的知识时，KD依然有效。我们进一步研究了特定领域、低资源场景，并提出一种两阶段KD策略：先利用合成教师标注数据，再在人工标注数据上进行精炼。该方法持续提升学生模型性能，为在数据稀缺环境中构建紧凑模型提供了实用指导。

summary: Objective: This study aims to improve the reliability and robustness of medical named entity recognition (NER) in Chinese atopic dermatitis (AD) clinical texts through explanation-guided learning. Methods: We propose a stability and boundary-aware explanation-guided NER framework. Perturbation-based analysis is used to evaluate explanation stability and entity boundary sensitivity. An adaptive fusion strategy dynamically combines local and global explanation to generate more reliable token-level explanations. The fused explanation signals are further incorporated into model training through stability, boundary-aware, and consistency constraints. Results: Experiments on Chinese AD NER datasets show that the proposed framework improves explanation robustness and achieves consistent performance gains across multiple NER models. The adaptive fusion strategy also provides more stable explanations and stronger boundary perception than individual explanation methods. Conclusion: The proposed method effectively integrates reliable explanation signals into medical NER training, improving both recognition performance and explanation reliability. The framework provides a practical and generalizable solution for explainable medical NER and offers reliable support for downstream clinical decision-making and medical knowledge applications.
title: Explanation-Guided Medical Named Entity Recognition with Stability and Boundary Awareness for Atopic Dermatitis
comment: Corresponding author: Xue Jiang, E-mail: xuejiang1025@126.com
title_translated: 基于解释引导的稳定性与边界感知医学命名实体识别用于特应性皮炎
summary_translated: 目标：本研究旨在通过解释引导学习提高中文特应性皮炎（AD）临床文本中医学命名实体识别（NER）的可靠性与鲁棒性。方法：我们提出一种稳定性和边界感知的解释引导NER框架。采用基于扰动的分析评估解释的稳定性与实体边界的敏感性。提出一种自适应融合策略，动态结合局部与全局解释，生成更可靠的细粒度解释。融合后的解释信号通过稳定性、边界感知及一致性约束进一步融入模型训练。结果：在中文AD NER数据集上的实验表明，所提框架提升了解释鲁棒性，并在多个NER模型上实现了稳定的性能提升。自适应融合策略相比单一解释方法，提供了更稳定且更强边界感知的解释。结论：所提方法有效将可靠的解释信号融入医学NER训练中，既提升了识别性能，也增强了解释可靠性。该框架为可解释医学NER提供了实用且可推广的解决方案，并为下游临床决策和医学知识应用提供可靠支持。

summary: LLM agents increasingly act as personal assistants that must remember a user's profile over months: who they are (attributes), what they routinely do (habits), and what they prefer (preferences), and keep it updated as jobs, routines, and tastes drift. Existing benchmarks evaluate this "memory" ability through short, simplified interactions, missing three core properties of real behavior: the profile is heterogeneous, with attributes, habits, and preferences evolving on different timelines; changes are driven by external context such as seasons and life events; and evidence is rarely stated explicitly, instead scattered across many small actions in different apps that a memory system must infer from. We introduce DynamicMem, a synthetic benchmark that constructs 15 months of activity per user, providing long-term multi-app data that real users' privacy keeps out of reach. It provides user-consistent trajectories averaging 2.2M tokens and 1,772 grounded events per user across 16 applications such as e-commerce, fitness, and social platforms. The profile evolves over this period and is never given explicitly: each attribute, habit, or preference must be inferred from small signals scattered across apps. We evaluate at five quarterly checkpoints to track how systems scale as history grows. Benchmarking five representative systems exposes problems a single accuracy score hides: (i) profile reconstruction degrades with history length while service-task accuracy stays flat, despite both drawing on the same memory; (ii) no system both keeps facts that stay true and replaces facts that change, with errors clustering on preferences and on naming the exact referent; and (iii) over 93% of failures trace to what the memory retrieves, not to the model writing the answer, so the largest room for improvement lies in memory itself. Code: https://wenyaxie023.github.io/DynamicMem/
title: DynamicMem: A Long-Horizon Memory Benchmark in Real-World Settings
title_translated: DynamicMem：真实场景下的长周期记忆基准测试
summary_translated: 大语言模型智能体越来越多地充当个人助理，需记住用户长达数月的个人资料：包括身份属性、日常习惯和偏好，并随工作、作息和口味的变化持续更新。现有基准通过简化的短期交互评估这种“记忆”能力，但忽略了真实行为的三个核心特征：资料本身具有异质性，属性、习惯和偏好在不同时间尺度上演化；变化由外部上下文（如季节和人生事件）驱动；证据极少明确陈述，而是分散在多个应用中的微小行为中，记忆系统必须从中推断。我们引入DynamicMem，一个合成基准，为每位用户构建长达15个月的活动数据，提供长期多应用数据，这是真实用户隐私所无法触及的。该基准为每位用户提供平均220万token和1772个有依据事件的连续轨迹，涵盖电商、健身、社交平台等16个应用。用户资料在此期间持续演化，且从未显式给出：每个属性、习惯或偏好都必须从跨应用的微小信号中推断。我们在五个季度检查点进行评估，以追踪系统随历史增长的表现。对五种代表性系统的基准测试揭示了单一准确率无法反映的问题：(i) 随着历史长度增加，资料重建性能下降，而服务任务准确率保持平稳，尽管二者均依赖同一记忆；(ii) 没有任何系统能同时保留不变的事实并替换已变化的事实，错误集中于偏好和精确指代物的命名；(iii) 超过93%的失败源于记忆检索内容，而非模型生成答案，因此最大的改进空间在于记忆机制本身。

summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve widespread integration across diverse linguistic landscapes, ensuring their safety and alignment with regional normative values remains a critical challenge. Current safety mechanisms are predominantly optimized for English-centric frameworks, often failing to capture the unique socio-cultural sensitivities and localized categories of harm inherent to the Indic region. To address this gap, we introduce IndicGuard, a multilingual safety guard model and dataset for Indic languages. We construct a high-volume, culturally nuanced safety dataset encompassing ten major Indic languages, systematically curated to capture regional harms, sensitive socio-political contexts, and adversarial jailbreaks. Leveraging this corpus, we fine-tune a 4B-parameter instruction-tuned model based on Gemma-3-4B-IT to serve as a multilingual safety guardrail for real-time content moderation and policy compliance checking. Our empirical evaluations demonstrate that IndicGuard significantly enhances LLM robustness against localized vulnerabilities, achieving high moderation consistency across different conversational turns. Crucially, IndicGuard consistently outperforms the existing baseline model, CultureGuard, across evaluated languages. Finally, we demonstrate that our model effectively generalizes to low-resource Indic languages excluded from training, substantiating the structural robustness and cross-lingual transfer capabilities of the framework.
title: IndicGuard: A Multilingual Safety Guard Model and Dataset for Indic Languages
title_translated: IndicGuard：印地语语言的安全防护模型与数据集
summary_translated: 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）在多样化的语言环境中广泛集成，确保其安全并符合区域规范价值仍是一项关键挑战。当前的安全机制主要针对以英语为中心的框架优化，往往无法捕捉印地地区特有的社会文化敏感性和局部伤害类别。为填补这一空白，我们提出了IndicGuard，一个面向印地语语言的多语言安全防护模型与数据集。我们构建了一个高容量、具有文化细微差别的安全数据集，涵盖十种主要印地语语言，系统性地收集了区域性危害、敏感的社会政治背景以及对抗性越狱攻击。基于该语料库，我们微调了一个基于Gemma-3-4B-IT的40亿参数指令微调模型，用作实时内容审核和政策合规检查的多语言安全护栏。实证评估表明，IndicGuard显著提升了LLM对本地化漏洞的鲁棒性，在不同对话轮次中实现了高度一致的内容审核效果。关键的是，IndicGuard在所有评估语言中均持续优于现有基线模型CultureGuard。最后，我们证明该模型能有效泛化到训练中未包含的低资源印地语语言，验证了该框架的结构鲁棒性与跨语言迁移能力。

summary: Classical TTS systems typically rely on rigid input formats and predefined metadata slots, limiting their ability to fulfill flexible user requirements. This paper introduces Bagpiper-TTS, a universal speech synthesis system that deals with diverse natural language user requests. Given a natural language prompt, Bagpiper-TTS first reasons over the users' intent to derive a rich caption, i.e., a comprehensive textual blueprint encompassing both transcription and nuanced metadata. Subsequently, this caption guides the synthesis of the target speech. Our model inherently supports a broad spectrum of tasks besides classical TTS applications, including multi-talker, intent-to-speech, role-play synthesis, singing voice synthesis, and more. Experimental results demonstrate that Bagpiper-TTS achieves an 1.7% Word Error Rate (WER) on the Seed-TTS-Eval benchmark and match the performance of dedicated models in both LLM-as-a-judge and human subjective evaluations across multiple applications.
title: Bagpiper-TTS: Natural Language Guided Universal Speech Synthesis
title_translated: Bagpiper-TTS：自然语言引导的通用语音合成
summary_translated: 传统TTS系统通常依赖于固定的输入格式和预定义的元数据字段，限制了其满足灵活用户需求的能力。本文提出Bagpiper-TTS，一种能够处理多样化自然语言用户请求的通用语音合成系统。给定一个自然语言提示，Bagpiper-TTS首先推理用户的意图，生成一个丰富的描述文本（即全面的文本蓝图，包含转录内容和细微的元数据）。随后，该描述指导目标语音的合成。我们的模型不仅支持经典TTS应用，还天然支持多种任务，包括多说话人合成、意图到语音、角色扮演合成、歌声合成等。实验结果表明，Bagpiper-TTS在Seed-TTS-Eval基准上达到1.7%的词错误率（WER），并在LLM作为裁判和人工主观评估中，于多个应用场景下表现媲美专用模型。

summary: As retrieval systems scale, high-quality reranking becomes increasingly important. However, most existing rerankers, whether encoder-based or decoder-based, jointly encode the query and passage, tightly coupling their computation and limiting deployment efficiency as well as flexibility. We present KaLM-Reranker-V1, a fast but not late-interaction (FBNL) reranker that decouples query and passage computation while retaining expressive relevance modeling. Built on an encoder-decoder architecture, KaLM-Reranker-V1 uses the encoder to pre-encode passages with Matryoshka embedding pooling, while the decoder models the system instruction, user instruction, and query intent; cross-attention then captures relevance between the query context and passage representations. This design makes KaLM-Reranker-V1 efficient through decoupled passage encoding, yet not late interaction, by preserving rich relevance modeling through cross-attention. We instantiate KaLM-Reranker-V1 in three sizes, Nano, Small, and Large, with 0.27B, 1B, and 4B activated parameters, respectively. Extensive experiments on BEIR, MIRACL, and LMEB demonstrate that KaLM-Reranker-V1 achieves strong reranking performance with superior efficiency. On BEIR, KaLM-Reranker-V1 achieves state-of-the-art performance, on par with strong industrial models such as the Qwen3-Reranker series; on MIRACL, despite not being extensively trained on multilingual data, KaLM-Reranker-V1 still shows excellent reranking performance. Moreover, on LMEB, reranking models demonstrate a clear advantage, with even the 0.27B Nano model remaining competitive with 7-12B embedding models.
title: KaLM-Reranker-V1: Fast but Not Late Interaction for Compressed Document Reranking
title_translated: KaLM-Reranker-V1：压缩文档重排序中的快速但非延迟交互
summary_translated: 随着检索系统的规模扩大，高质量的重排序变得愈发重要。然而，大多数现有重排序器，无论是基于编码器还是解码器的，都会联合编码查询与段落，紧密耦合其计算过程，从而限制了部署效率与灵活性。我们提出KaLM-Reranker-V1，一种快速但非延迟交互（FBNL）的重排序器，通过解耦查询与段落的计算，同时保留强大的相关性建模能力。该模型基于编码器-解码器架构，使用编码器对段落进行预编码，并采用马特里什卡嵌入池化技术；解码器则建模系统指令、用户指令和查询意图；交叉注意力机制捕捉查询上下文与段落表示之间的相关性。这种设计使KaLM-Reranker-V1在解耦段落编码的同时，通过交叉注意力保持丰富的相关性建模能力，从而实现高效运行。我们实例化了三种尺寸的模型（Nano、Small、Large），分别具有0.27B、1B和4B激活参数。在BEIR、MIRACL和LMEB上的大量实验表明，KaLM-Reranker-V1在性能和效率方面均表现出色。在BEIR上，其表现达到业界领先水平，与Qwen3-Reranker系列等强大工业模型相当；在MIRACL上，尽管未在多语言数据上进行充分训练，仍展现出优异的重排序性能。此外，在LMEB上，重排序模型优势明显，即使是0.27B的Nano模型也具备与7-12B嵌入模型竞争的能力。

summary: In sparse Mixture-of-Experts language models, does the same token id imply the same router state and the same experts producing it? Holding the emitted token id fixed at repeated anchors, we find it does not: the experts that produce it still separate task context, trajectory history, and reasoning-effort mode. This residual structure supports test-time control: near \emph{boundary} anchors (the final-response transition) and \emph{delimiter} anchors (which open the answer, e.g.\ \texttt{\textbackslash boxed\{} or code fences), routing neighborhoods already align with final-answer basins at a marker-only readout and strongest when the routing is read at the answer opening. We operationalize this as \textbf{RAD} (Routing Agreement Decoding), an answer-string-free multi-rollout selector: it locates a fixed anchor, represents each rollout by its anchor-window MoE routing states, and returns the densest Weighted-Jaccard $K$-NN route-basin center, without parsing, normalizing, executing, or voting over answer strings. Across 10 sparse-MoE configurations (gpt-oss, Qwen3-MoE) and 6 datasets spanning math, GPQA, and code, RAD is on par with Majority where string voting is well-posed, with small positive paired deltas (RAD $73.9$ / RAD+DC $74.2$ vs.\ Majority $73.6$). Like majority voting, RAD is not a verifier: a dense \emph{wrong} basin can still win. Its value is the interface: the same selector gives direct pass@1 on code, where exact-string voting is ill-defined, and the same routing-density principle, re-anchored to the agentic boundary, improves best-of-16 patch selection on SWE-bench Verified over random, where patches have no answer string to vote on.
title: Does the Same Token Mean the Same State? MoE Routing as Signal for Reasoning Control
title_translated: 同一个标记是否意味着同一状态？MoE路由作为推理控制信号
summary_translated: 在稀疏的专家混合（MoE）语言模型中，相同的标记ID是否意味着相同的路由器状态和相同的专家产生它？在固定重复锚点处的输出标记ID时，我们发现并非如此：产生该标记的专家仍然区分任务上下文、轨迹历史和推理努力模式。这种残余结构支持测试时的控制：在接近“边界”锚点（最终响应转换点）和“分隔符”锚点（如打开答案的标记，例如\boxed{}或代码块分隔符）时，路由邻域已与最终答案基底对齐，且在仅读取标记时即达到最强效果，尤其在答案开启时刻读取路由信息时表现最佳。我们将其操作化为**RAD**（路由一致性解码），一种无需答案字符串的多轮次选择器：它定位固定锚点，用每个轮次的锚点窗口内MoE路由状态表示各轮次，返回最密集的加权Jaccard K-NN路由基底中心，无需解析、归一化、执行或对答案字符串投票。在10个稀疏MoE配置（gpt-oss, Qwen3-MoE）和6个涵盖数学、GPQA及代码的基准数据集上，RAD的表现与多数投票法相当，仅有小幅正向差异（RAD 73.9 / RAD+DC 74.2 vs. Majority 73.6）。与多数投票类似，RAD并非验证器：一个密集的“错误”基底仍可能胜出。其价值在于接口：相同的选取器可直接提供代码任务的pass@1指标，其中精确字符串投票不适用；同样的路由密度原则，重新锚定至智能体边界，可提升SWE-bench Verified上最佳16个补丁的选择效果，优于随机选择，而这些补丁并无可供投票的答案字符串。

summary: Understanding moral values in social media text offers insight into moral judgement formation, and supervised NLP models trained on crowdsourced data have achieved strong classification performance. However, most approaches simplify the problem by aggregating multiple annotators' labels into a single "ground truth", overlooking the inherent subjectivity of the task. In practice, there are disagreements between annotators caused by personal viewpoint or inherent ambiguities, particularly for short tweets. Here, we extend a pretrained language model with a layer that learns annotator-specific features. Our model improves predictions of individual annotations and yields representations that reveal meaningful insights into annotators' moral perspectives. We show that models trained on aggregated labels may hide variation and give a misleading impression of performance. Overall, we demonstrate that disagreement reflects the inherent subjectivity of the task and that modelling individual perspectives creates benefits for moral classification of texts.
title: Learning Moral Diversity: Modelling Individual Perspectives in Moral Classification of Texts
comment: Accepted at the Seventh Workshop on NLP and Computational Social Science. 12 pages, 7 figures
title_translated: 学习道德多样性：文本道德分类中的个体视角建模
summary_translated: 理解社交媒体文本中的道德价值观有助于揭示道德判断的形成过程。基于众包数据训练的监督NLP模型在文本分类任务中已取得良好表现。然而，大多数方法通过将多个标注者的标签聚合为单一“真实标签”来简化问题，忽略了该任务固有的主观性。实际上，由于个人观点或内在模糊性，特别是在短推文场景中，标注者之间常存在分歧。本文通过在预训练语言模型上增加一层，学习标注者特定的特征，扩展了模型能力。我们的模型提升了对个体标注的预测准确性，并生成了揭示标注者道德视角的有意义表征。我们指出，基于聚合标签训练的模型可能掩盖差异，造成性能表现的误导性印象。总体而言，我们证明了分歧反映了任务的固有主观性，而建模个体视角能为文本道德分类带来实际益处。

summary: Some professional authors are beginning to use AI tools to help produce their fiction writing. Are readers using AI to generate fiction, too? This paper examines how large language models are reshaping the production and consumption of fiction by enabling new forms of participation in narrative generation. Drawing on over 500,000 anonymized, English-language ChatGPT-user conversations (arXiv:2405.01470), we find that more than one third of the conversations involve some form of fiction generation -- including original stories, roleplay, fanfiction, and erotica. This AI-generated fiction is notably dominated by power users. We identify common fiction generation patterns and profiles among these users, including what we call "infinite story demanders," who repeatedly request and revise variations of the same or similar narratives over extended periods of time. We show that users especially gravitate toward fanfiction and erotica, and that they are broadly drawn to generic forms, repetition, immediacy, and niche combinations of story elements. Our findings motivate two theoretical provocations. First, we argue that AI technologies may lead to a shift in the conventional relationship between the author and reader, potentially producing what we call a "solipsistic reader-writer," who both generates and consumes fiction within a closed conversational loop, interacting with a machine rather than a human other. Second, we note that LLMs enable interactivity, play, and permutation in ways that are seemingly pleasurable for users, raising questions about where AI will fit into contemporary storytelling and entertainment ecosystems. We situate these developments within broader transformations in literature and media, including self-publishing, fanfiction, and pornography, and suggest that AI-generated fiction shares structural affinities with on-demand, personalized, and repetitive cultural forms.
title: AI Fiction in the Wild
comment: Presented at the MFS Cultural AI Conference, Purdue University, September 19, 2025. This essay is provisionally forthcoming in MFS: Modern Fiction Studies
title_translated: 野生中的AI小说
summary_translated: 一些专业作家开始使用AI工具辅助创作小说。读者是否也在用AI生成小说？本文探讨了大语言模型如何通过促进叙事生成中的新参与形式，重塑小说的生产和消费方式。基于超过50万条匿名英文ChatGPT用户对话数据（arXiv:2405.01470），我们发现超过三分之一的对话涉及某种形式的小说生成——包括原创故事、角色扮演、同人小说和情色内容。这些AI生成的小说主要由高活跃用户主导。我们识别出这些用户的常见创作模式与画像，包括所谓的“无限故事需求者”，他们长时间反复请求并修改相同或相似的叙事变体。研究显示，用户尤其偏好同人小说和情色内容，且普遍倾向于通用形式、重复性、即时性和特定元素的组合。我们的发现引发两个理论性思考：第一，我们认为AI技术可能改变作者与读者之间的传统关系，可能导致一种“自恋型读者-作者”出现，即个体在封闭的对话循环中既生成又消费小说，互动对象是机器而非人类；第二，我们注意到LLM能够实现交互性、游戏性和排列组合，这种特性对用户而言似乎具有愉悦感，从而引发关于AI在当代叙事与娱乐生态系统中定位的疑问。我们将这些发展置于更广泛的文学与媒体变革背景中，包括自助出版、同人小说和色情内容，并指出AI生成小说在结构上与按需、个性化和重复性的文化形式具有相似性。

summary: This study investigates sentiment polarity biases, specifically, differences in how accurately AI models classify positive versus negative reviews across languages and model architectures. Large language models show a negative bias in French and are more accurate on negative reviews, while encoder models exhibit positive bias in Japanese, missing negative reviews that use indirect criticism. These language-specific polarity biases have implications in both social and business domains deploying multilingual sentiment analysis systems.
title: Language-Specific Sentiment Polarity Biases in Encoder and Large Language Model Classification of Product Reviews
title_translated: 编码器与大语言模型在产品评论情感极性分类中的语言特异性偏差
summary_translated: 本研究调查了情感极性偏差，具体分析了AI模型在不同语言和模型架构下对正面与负面评论分类准确性的差异。大语言模型在法语中表现出负向偏差，对负面评论的分类更为准确；而编码器模型在日语中则表现出正向偏差，容易遗漏使用间接批评的负面评论。这些语言特异性的极性偏差在部署多语言情感分析系统的社会与商业领域具有重要影响。
