summary_translated: 大语言模型（LLMs）在对齐目标价值函数方面已取得显著进展。我们认为，即使在训练后模型已良好对齐，其在推理过程中仍可能无法最大化对齐后的价值。我们从数学上形式化了这一差距，定义为‘理性价值风险’：即模型部署时的推理策略与其理性对应物（即沿最陡方向最大化期望效用的响应）之间的效用差异。该风险的估计误差进一步分解为三个部分：有限候选集、有限提示和不完美验证器的影响。我们在多个模型（Llama-3.1、Qwen-2.5、T{"}ulu-3系列（7B-72B）、GPT-5.2、GPT-5.5、DeepSeek-V4）和基准测试（UltraFeedback、AlpacaEval、GSM8K、MATH、HumanEval、MathArena）上进行了广泛实验。结果验证了：（1）理性价值风险普遍存在；（2）价值对齐可降低但无法消除该风险；（3）该风险对推理阶段的策略高度敏感；（4）更长的推理过程能提升理性，但收益递减。代码已公开。

summary: How do people build abstract, reusable knowledge from sequential experience under bounded cognitive resources? To answer this question, we integrate rate-distortion theory with recent advances in program induction to describe how prior knowledge shapes which future structures are cheap to encode and easy to discover. We formalize this in a hierarchical Adaptor Grammar (HAG) with distinct local (within-task) and global (across-task) libraries, governed jointly by constraints on memory and computation. In simulations, HAG achieves better rate-distortion trade-offs and stronger generalization than fixed grammars or shallow chunking methods. In an online melodic sequence-learning experiment, participants' recall errors reflected systematic simplifications and reaction times increased at inferred program boundaries. Trial-by-trial fits further showed that hierarchical libraries best explained individual differences in both recall and out-of-sample continuation choices, outperforming all alternative models. These findings cast structured learning as bounded program induction in which the order of experience shapes future abstractions a learner builds.
title: Path-dependent program induction under resource constraints explains human sequence learning
title_translated: 资源约束下的路径依赖程序归纳解释人类序列学习
summary_translated: 人们如何在认知资源有限的情况下，从序列经验中构建抽象且可复用的知识？为回答此问题，我们将速率失真理论与近期程序归纳进展相结合，描述先验知识如何影响未来结构的编码成本和发现难易程度。我们提出一种分层适配器语法（HAG），包含独立的局部（任务内）和全局（跨任务）库，由记忆与计算约束共同调控。模拟结果显示，HAG在速率失真权衡和泛化能力上优于固定语法或浅层分块方法。在一项在线旋律序列学习实验中，参与者回忆错误呈现出系统性简化特征，反应时间在推断出的程序边界处增加。逐次试验拟合表明，分层库能最好地解释个体在回忆和样本外延续选择中的差异，优于所有替代模型。这些发现将结构化学习视为受限的程序归纳过程，其中经验顺序决定了学习者未来构建的抽象形态。

summary: Multi-agent debate improves the reliability of large language models (LLMs) through iterative peer critiques. However, fixed topologies often introduce persistent positional biases, amplify unreliable agents, and cause high sensitivity to role assignments. We introduce \textit{Permutation-Equivariant Adaptive Routing Multi-Agent Debate (PEAR)}, an inference-time protocol that dynamically reconfigures communication roles and sparse topologies across consecutive debate rounds. By strategically switching agent-to-role assignments based on evolving agent states, PEAR prevents any agent from permanently occupying a privileged network position or distributes influence more evenly across the debate. We theoretically characterize PEAR as an equivariant sparse router: it preserves accuracy under agent relabeling while reducing routing complexity and improving generalization. Comprehensive empirical evaluations across four reasoning benchmarks and six diverse LLM backbones demonstrate PEAR significantly improves average accuracy over the strongest debate baselines. The code is at https://github.com/EVIEHub/PEAR.
title: PEAR: Permutation-Equivariant Adaptive Routing Multi-Agent Debate
title_translated: PEAR：置换等变自适应路由多智能体辩论
summary_translated: 多智能体辩论通过迭代同行评审提升了大语言模型（LLMs）的可靠性。然而，固定拓扑结构常引入持久的位置偏差，放大不可靠智能体的影响，并使系统对角色分配高度敏感。我们提出	extit{置换等变自适应路由多智能体辩论（PEAR）}，一种在推理阶段动态重构通信角色和稀疏拓扑的协议，适用于连续辩论回合。通过根据智能体状态演变策略性切换角色分配，PEAR防止任何智能体永久占据特权网络位置，或更均衡地分配辩论影响力。我们从理论上将PEAR刻画为一种等变稀疏路由机制：在智能体重命名下保持准确性的同时，降低路由复杂度并提升泛化能力。在四个推理基准和六种不同LLM骨干上的全面实证评估表明，PEAR显著优于最强的辩论基线，大幅提升了平均准确率。代码已开源。

summary: Today's agents are highly effective at implementing well-scoped software design plans, but user intent is often vague and admits multiple equally valid solutions. In this paper, we introduce SpecBench, a new benchmark for evaluating an agent's ability to translate user intent into a structured, executable specification that aligns with user preferences. The agent is given access to past user conversations and may interact with the user for a fixed number of rounds to ask clarifying questions. We find that existing agents exhibit two extreme behaviors: they either (i) struggle to collaborate proactively with users, entering implementation mode too quickly while overestimating their understanding of user preferences, or (ii) exhaust their question budget by asking about every ambiguous design choice. To address this limitation, we introduce a user-assistant agent: Buddy. It follows a workflow inspired by classical morphological analysis, decomposing user intent into a structured space of design dimensions and candidate choices. It then creates simulated users to evaluate these choices, before engaging the real user to resolve remaining ambiguities and finalize the specification. By shifting the focus from execution to specification, SpecBench and Buddy emphasize agent-user collaboration (not just code generation) as a key frontier in future agent design.
title: Turning Intent into Specifications: A Benchmark and an Interactive User-Assistant Agent
title_translated: 从意图到规范：一个基准测试与交互式用户助手智能体
summary_translated: 当前的智能体在执行明确范围的软件设计计划方面非常高效，但用户意图往往模糊，且存在多个同样合理的解决方案。本文提出SpecBench，一个用于评估智能体将用户意图转化为结构化、可执行规范能力的新基准测试。该智能体可访问过往用户对话，并可在固定轮次内与用户互动以提出澄清问题。我们发现现有智能体表现出两种极端行为：要么（i）难以主动与用户协作，过早进入实现模式，高估自己对用户偏好的理解；要么（ii）耗尽提问预算，对每个模糊的设计选择都进行询问。为解决这一局限，我们引入了一个用户助手智能体——Buddy。它采用经典形态分析的流程，将用户意图分解为设计维度和候选方案的结构化空间，随后通过模拟用户评估这些方案，在此基础上与真实用户互动以解决剩余歧义并最终确定规范。通过将重点从执行转向规范，SpecBench与Buddy强调了智能体-用户协作（而不仅仅是代码生成）是未来智能体设计的关键前沿。
